title: What Foundry Agent Service Is
summary: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service is the managed platform for building, deploying, scaling, and observing AI agents. An agent combines a Foundry model, instructions, tools, and state so it can reason, call tools, and complete multi-step work.
analogy: Calling a model directly is like asking a smart officer one question over a radio. An agent is like assigning that officer a mission binder, approved equipment, a logbook, and a command channel that stays open until the mission is complete.
diagram:
graph TD
User[User] --> Agent[Foundry agent]
Agent --> Model[Deployed model]
Agent --> Instructions[Instructions]
Agent --> Tools[Tools]
Agent --> State[Conversation state]
Agent --> Trace[Telemetry]
body:
## First principles

First principles: a model predicts the next useful text, but a real assistant must remember a task, follow standing instructions, choose tools, authenticate safely, and continue across steps. Foundry Agent Service adds a managed runtime around a deployed model so the model can operate as an application component instead of a one-shot text function.

## Definition

Microsoft Foundry Agent Service is the managed platform for building, deploying, scaling, and observing AI agents. An agent combines a Foundry model, instructions, tools, and state so it can reason, call tools, and complete multi-step work.

## How it fits together

Think model plus policy plus tools plus memory. The model decides; instructions constrain; tools act; state remembers; tracing explains what happened later.

## Worked example

```python
# pip install azure-ai-projects>=2.0.0 azure-identity
# Uses DefaultAzureCredential so local development can use az login and Azure hosts can use managed identity.
import os
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectClient
from azure.ai.projects.models import PromptAgentDefinition

project = AIProjectClient(
    endpoint=os.environ["PROJECT_ENDPOINT"],
    credential=DefaultAzureCredential(),
)

agent = project.agents.create_version(
    agent_name=os.environ.get("AGENT_NAME", "foundry-agent-basics"),
    definition=PromptAgentDefinition(
        model=os.environ.get("MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME", "gpt-5-mini"),
        instructions="Explain Azure AI concepts with short examples and ask one check question.",
    ),
)
print(agent.name, agent.version, agent.id)
```

*Create a prompt agent using the current Azure AI Projects 2.x shape*

drills.q: What turns a model call into an agent?
drills.a: Persistent instructions, state, tools, identity, lifecycle, and observability around the model.

drills.q: Why not call the model directly for everything?
drills.a: Direct calls are simpler, but they do not provide the same managed tool orchestration, state, publishing, identity, and tracing.

drills.q: What are the three core components Microsoft lists for an agent?

teachItBack: An agent is a managed AI application shell around a deployed model. It remembers a conversation, follows standing instructions, and invokes approved tools. Use direct model calls for one-step text generation and agents for governed multi-step work.

glossary.definition: A model-backed application unit with instructions, tools, and state.
A configuration-defined agent that Foundry runs without your own runtime code.
A preview code-based agent hosted by Foundry in managed compute.

sources.title: Microsoft Learn: What is Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
Microsoft Learn: Foundry quickstart with Azure AI Projects 2.x

quiz.q: What is the best first-principles definition of a Foundry agent?
choices: A model plus instructions, tools, state, identity, and runtime lifecycle
A static prompt saved in a text file
A personal access token for a model
quiz.explanation: An agent wraps a model with instructions, tools, state, and managed runtime behavior.
